texto: To conquer your biology, stop seeing people sexually and start seeing them as family. In our day, we have really a perverted notion where it's like strangers and potential sexual objects.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Driscoll: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “To conquer your biology, stop seeing people sexually and start seeing them as family. In our day,…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, church, community pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de community e sex.

texto: While it only takes one spouse to be friendly, it takes both spouses to be friends. When both spouses are unfriendly, the marriage is marked by conflict and coldness. When one spouse is friendly and the other is unfriendly, the marriage is marked by selfishness and sadness. But when both spouses each make a deep, heartfelt covenant with God to continually seek to become a better friend, increasing love and laughter mark the marriage.

texto: Religious people are not fun... So, Jesus shows up and kids run around Him, want to be with Him. You know why? He's fun... God's a Father who likes His kids to have fun, so when Jesus shows up, the religious people get jealous because Jesus gets invited to parties and the religious people don't.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Mark Driscoll que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Religious people are not fun...
